meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Build a Portfolio: Email Automation Guide for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building real-world email automation projects. A beginner's guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your career.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends by shipping functional automation. By building an email automation tool using Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate technical competency, system architecture skills, and the ability to solve real business problems—turning your lack of experience into a showcase of high-impact AI engineering capability.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and the daunting reality hits: every job description demands three years of experience you haven't accrued yet. You are stuck in the 'no portfolio' paradox, where you need a job to get experience, but you need experience to get a job. It is a cycle designed to keep beginners on the sidelines.

But the rules have changed. Today, you don't need a decade of tenure to prove your worth—you need a product. We are going to bypass the traditional gatekeepers by building a production-grade email automation system. This isn't a classroom exercise; it is a tangible, automated machine that solves a real-world pain point, proving you are not just a student, but an AI builder.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the trigger-action flow. We will build a system that monitors a specific data source and triggers personalized emails.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a schema for an email automation system that monitors a CSV input, formats personalized outreach, and logs status to a JSON file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a project structure for an automated email sender with config.json and main.py'

steps.title: Initialize the Environment
steps.description: Set up a clean, modular environment where your automation logic can reside without polluting global dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a requirements.txt and a boilerplate script for an SMTP email client using Python's smtplib.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize a virtual environment and install necessary libraries for email automation'

steps.title: Implement the Data Parser
steps.description: Create a robust parser that reads your contact list and prepares the data for the AI-generated email body.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Python function to read a CSV file with columns: name, email, and company_context, returning a list of dictionaries.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement a data parsing module to safely read user inputs'

steps.title: Integrate AI Personalization
steps.description: Use an LLM to generate unique email content for each recipient, significantly increasing your automation's value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a function that takes company_context and uses an LLM API to generate a professional, concise introductory email.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add an AI integration module to generate personalized email body content'

steps.title: Automate the Delivery
steps.description: Wire the sender module to execute the emails, ensuring you include error handling for failed deliveries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust main loop that iterates through the parsed contacts, sends emails, and logs errors to a local file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create the main execution loop with error handling and logging'

pro_tips: Always implement a 'dry-run' flag in your code to print to console instead of sending real emails during testing.
Use environment variables (.env) for all API keys; never hardcode credentials, even in a portfolio project.
Add a 'Rate Limiter' to your loop to respect SMTP provider limits and avoid getting your IP blacklisted.
Include a 'README.md' that explains the 'Why' behind your architecture, not just the 'How'.
Use a professional testing suite like pytest to validate your data parsing logic; employers love seeing unit tests.
content.next_steps: Once your script is sending emails reliably, wrap it in a Docker container to demonstrate DevOps proficiency, then document the results on your GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to build a real-world tool that separates you from the crowd. Don't let this knowledge sit idle; ship it, document it, and claim your place as a developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
